# fasthtml


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## Tests

Route and rendering tests run against the FastHTML app with a stubbed
kernel — no server process, no browser. They come first so the exported
cells below stay honest.

``` python
import json
from urllib.parse import quote
from fasthtml.common import to_xml
from starlette.testclient import TestClient
import paar.bridge as B, paar.fasthtml as F
from paar.fasthtml import app, _node, _broadcast, _acc
from paar.core import VarInfo

class _IP:
    def __init__(self, ns): self.user_ns=ns; self.user_ns_hidden=set()
    class events:
        @staticmethod
        def register(n, fn): pass

def test_node_scalar_no_toggle():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='n', type='int', value='5', accessor=('n',), path='n')))
    assert 'n' in html and 'int' in html and '5' in html
    assert '/expand' not in html and '<details' not in html
def test_node_container_details():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='d', type='dict', value='{...}',
                                is_container=True, accessor=('d',), path='d')))
    assert '<details' in html and '<summary' in html
    assert 'paar-children' in html and '/expand?accessor=' in html
def test_node_container_lazy_once():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='d', type='dict', is_container=True, accessor=('d',), path='d')))
    assert 'once' in html   # hx-trigger="click once" -> children fetched only on first open
def test_node_more_sentinel():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='…', type='', value='150 more', accessor=('L',), path='L', more_offset=100)))
    assert '/expand?accessor=' in html and 'offset=100' in html and '150 more' in html
    assert 'hx-target="this"' in html and '<details' not in html   # clicking swaps itself for the next page
def test_rows_route_lists_vars():
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'alpha': 123})
    r = TestClient(app).get('/rows?profile=standard')
    assert r.status_code==200 and 'alpha' in r.text and '123' in r.text and 'id="rows"' in r.text
def test_home_wires_ws_and_loader():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert r.status_code==200
    assert 'ws-connect="/live"' in r.text and 'hx-ext="ws"' in r.text
    assert 'id="rows"' in r.text and '/rows' in r.text
def test_home_has_profile_select():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert '<select' in r.text and 'name="profile"' in r.text
    assert 'minimal' in r.text and 'standard' in r.text   # profile options rendered
def test_rows_profile_filters_and_groups():
    import math
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'myvar': 5, 'mymod': math})
    mn = TestClient(app).get('/rows?profile=minimal').text
    assert 'myvar' in mn and 'Modules' not in mn          # minimal hides the module group
    st = TestClient(app).get('/rows?profile=standard').text
    assert 'myvar' in st and 'Modules' in st and 'mymod' in st   # standard groups modules
def test_expand_route_returns_children():
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'d': {'x': 1}})
    r = TestClient(app).get('/expand?accessor=' + quote(json.dumps(['d'])))
    assert r.status_code==200 and 'x' in r.text and '1' in r.text
def test_expand_route_paging():
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'L': list(range(250))})
    r = TestClient(app).get('/expand?accessor=' + quote(json.dumps(['L'])) + '&offset=100')
    assert r.status_code==200 and '100' in r.text and 'offset=200' in r.text   # trailing load-more points at page 3
def test_broadcast_drops_dead_clients():
    F._clients.clear()
    def _bad_send(_): raise RuntimeError('dead')
    F._clients.append(('not-a-loop', _bad_send))
    _broadcast('x')
    assert F._clients==[]
def test_node_grid_only_toggles_grid():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='arr', type='ndarray', is_grid=True, accessor=('arr',), path='arr')))
    assert '<details' in html and '/grid?accessor=' in html and 'paar-gridbox' in html
    assert '/expand' not in html   # not a container -> the row toggles the grid, no tree
def test_node_grid_and_container_shows_both():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='arr', type='ndarray', is_grid=True, is_container=True,
                                accessor=('arr',), path='arr')))
    assert '/expand?accessor=' in html and '/grid?accessor=' in html   # tree + grid coexist
    assert 'paar-gridbox' in html and 'paar-children' in html and 'paar-gridtoggle' in html
    assert html.count('<details') >= 2 and html.count('click once') >= 2   # both are lazy toggles
def test_grid_route_renders_table():
    import numpy as np
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'arr': np.arange(6).reshape(2,3)})
    r = TestClient(app).get('/grid?accessor=' + quote(json.dumps(['arr'])) + '&roff=0&coff=0')
    assert r.status_code==200 and '<table' in r.text and '5' in r.text  # last cell value present
def test_exec_route_assignment():
    import paar.runtime as R, types
    class _Sh:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns={}
        def run_cell(s, code, store_history=True):
            exec(code, s.user_ns)
            return types.SimpleNamespace(result=None, error_in_exec=None, error_before_exec=None, success=True)
    sh = _Sh(); R.get_ipython = lambda: sh
    r = TestClient(app).post('/exec', data={'code':'q = 5'})
    assert r.status_code == 200 and sh.user_ns['q'] == 5 and 'exec-out' in r.text
def test_exec_bar_renders():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert 'hx-post="/exec"' in r.text and 'id="paar-code"' in r.text
    assert 'name="code"' in r.text and 'isolated' in r.text   # hidden textarea still named code
def test_editor_script_present():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert 'codejar' in r.text.lower() and 'shiftKey' in r.text   # editor loaded + shift+enter binding
def test_edit_route_returns_form():
    r = TestClient(app).post('/edit', data={'accessor': json.dumps(['x'])})
    assert r.status_code == 200 and 'hx-post="/set"' in r.text and 'name="expr"' in r.text
def test_set_route_writes_and_refreshes():
    import paar.runtime as R
    class _IP2:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns={'x':1}; s.user_ns_hidden=set()
        class events:
            @staticmethod
            def register(n, fn): pass
    ip = _IP2(); B.get_ipython = lambda: ip; R.get_ipython = lambda: ip
    F._clients.clear()
    r = TestClient(app).post('/set', data={'accessor': json.dumps(['x']), 'expr':'42'})
    assert r.status_code == 200 and ip.user_ns['x'] == 42 and 'id="rows"' in r.text
def test_set_route_error_inline():
    import paar.runtime as R
    class _IP3:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns={'s':{1,2}}; s.user_ns_hidden=set()
        class events:
            @staticmethod
            def register(n, fn): pass
    ip = _IP3(); B.get_ipython = lambda: ip; R.get_ipython = lambda: ip
    r = TestClient(app).post('/set', data={'accessor': json.dumps(['s',0]), 'expr':'9'})
    assert r.status_code == 200 and 'exec-out' in r.text   # error surfaced OOB, tree intact
def test_node_value_is_editable():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='x', type='int', value='1', accessor=('x',), path='x')))
    assert '/edit?accessor=' in html and 'paar-val' in html
def test_nodes_have_data_attrs():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='arr', type='ndarray', value='x', accessor=('arr',), path='arr')))
    assert 'data-name="arr"' in html and 'data-type="ndarray"' in html
def test_filter_bar_renders():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert 'paar-filter-name' in r.text and 'paar-filter-type' in r.text
def test_exec_result_not_editable():
    html = to_xml(_node(VarInfo(name='result', type='int', value='7', accessor=('_',), path='_')))
    assert 'paar-val' not in html and '/edit?accessor=' not in html   # `_` result row is not click-to-edit
def test_complete_route_json():
    import paar.runtime as R
    class _Sh:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns={'alpha':1,'alto':2}
        def complete(s, text=None, line=None, cursor_pos=None):
            import re
            q = text if text is not None else (line or '')[:cursor_pos]
            tok = re.split(r'[^\w.]', q)[-1]
            return tok, [k for k in s.user_ns if k.startswith(tok)]
    R.get_ipython=lambda: _Sh()
    r = TestClient(app).get('/complete?code=al&pos=2')
    assert r.status_code==200
    j = r.json(); assert j['from']==0 and set(j['matches'])=={'alpha','alto'}
def test_editor_has_autocomplete_and_clear():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert '/complete?code=' in r.text                 # editor fetches completions
    assert 'htmx:afterRequest' in r.text               # clear-on-run wired
    assert 'paar-ac' in r.text                          # completion popup styling present
def test_sessions_and_session_routes():
    import paar.runtime as R, tempfile, types
    from pathlib import Path
    R.SESSION_DIR = Path(tempfile.mkdtemp())
    class _Sh:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns={}
        def run_cell(s, code, store_history=True):
            exec(code, s.user_ns)
            return types.SimpleNamespace(result=None, error_in_exec=None, error_before_exec=None, success=True)
    R.get_ipython=lambda: _Sh()
    R.run('zz = 5')
    assert 'session_' in TestClient(app).get('/sessions').text
    name = R.list_sessions()[0][0]
    s2 = TestClient(app).get('/session?name='+name).text
    assert 'zz = 5' in s2 and 'paar-reuse' in s2
def test_home_has_sessions_panel():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert 'Sessions' in r.text and '/sessions' in r.text
def test_editor_exposes_paarload():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/')
    assert 'paarLoad' in r.text and 'paar-reuse' in r.text
def test_sessions_lists_current():
    r = TestClient(app).get('/sessions')
    assert 'current' in r.text   # current session always shown at top
for t in [test_node_scalar_no_toggle,test_node_container_details,test_node_container_lazy_once,
          test_node_more_sentinel,test_rows_route_lists_vars,test_home_wires_ws_and_loader,
          test_home_has_profile_select,test_rows_profile_filters_and_groups,
          test_expand_route_returns_children,test_expand_route_paging,
          test_broadcast_drops_dead_clients,test_node_grid_only_toggles_grid,
          test_node_grid_and_container_shows_both,test_grid_route_renders_table,
          test_exec_route_assignment,test_exec_bar_renders,test_editor_script_present,
          test_edit_route_returns_form,test_set_route_writes_and_refreshes,
          test_set_route_error_inline,test_node_value_is_editable,
          test_nodes_have_data_attrs,test_filter_bar_renders,
          test_exec_result_not_editable,
          test_complete_route_json,test_editor_has_autocomplete_and_clear,
          test_sessions_and_session_routes,test_home_has_sessions_panel,
          test_editor_exposes_paarload,test_sessions_lists_current]: t()
```

``` python
# serve() + JSON API (frontend-agnostic feed for paar.tui) + env discovery
import os, tempfile
os.environ['PAAR_REG_DIR'] = tempfile.mkdtemp()   # keep registry writes out of ~/.paar during tests

def test_api_rows_json():
    import math
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'alpha': 123, 'mymod': math})
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/rows?profile=standard').json()
    assert j['profile']=='standard' and 'standard' in j['profiles']
    names = [n['name'] for g in j['groups'] for n in g['nodes']]
    assert 'alpha' in names and 'Modules' in [g['label'] for g in j['groups']]
def test_api_expand_json():
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'d': {'x': 1}})
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/expand?accessor=' + quote(json.dumps(['d']))).json()
    assert any(n['name']=="'x'" for n in j) and any(n['value']=='1' for n in j)
def test_api_grid_json():
    import numpy as np
    B.get_ipython = lambda: _IP({'arr': np.arange(6).reshape(2,3)})
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/grid?accessor=' + quote(json.dumps(['arr']))).json()
    assert j['nrows']==2 and j['ncols']==3 and j['cells'][1][2]=='5'
def test_api_envs_json():
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/envs').json()
    assert 'env' in j and isinstance(j['envs'], list)
def test_api_exec_json():
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/exec?code=1%2B1').json()   # 1+1 (url-encoded +)
    assert set(('ok','result','stdout','error')) <= set(j)   # shape present (result None without a live kernel)
def test_api_sessions_json():
    j = TestClient(app).get('/api/sessions').json()
    assert 'current' in j and isinstance(j['sessions'], list) and j['sessions'][0]['current']
    js = TestClient(app).get('/api/session?name=nope').json()
    assert js['cells'] == []   # unknown/missing session -> empty
def test_serve_wires_ns_and_registry():
    class _Fake:
        def __init__(self, app, port, daemon): self.port=port
    orig, F._server = F.JupyUvi, None
    F.JupyUvi = _Fake
    try:
        url = F.serve(port=8765, ns={'sv': 1}, name='unit', poll=999, agent_kernel=False)
        assert url.endswith(':8765') and F._env=='unit'
        rows = TestClient(app).get('/api/rows').json()
        assert 'sv' in [n['name'] for g in rows['groups'] for n in g['nodes']]   # inspects the passed ns
        assert 'unit' in [e['name'] for e in F.R.active()]                        # registered for discovery
    finally:
        F.JupyUvi = orig; F.R.unregister(F._port)
        F._server = F._port = F._env = None; F.set_namespace(None)
for t in [test_api_rows_json, test_api_expand_json, test_api_grid_json,
          test_api_envs_json, test_api_exec_json, test_api_sessions_json, test_serve_wires_ns_and_registry]: t()
```

Agent-surface and owner-token tests: the overlay creates without
touching the owner namespace, the policy blocks mutations at the HTTP
boundary, `readonly` forces isolated exec, `off` 403s, and
`/exec`//`/api/exec` demand the token exactly when one is armed.

``` python
# owner-token gating + the restricted /agent/api surface
os.environ['PAAR_INSPECTORS'] = '/nonexistent-inspectors.py'   # keep user rules files out of tests

def test_agent_exec_creates_and_reads():
    F._agent = None; F._agent_mode = 'restricted'
    ip = _IP({'base': 10, 'items': [1, 2]})
    B.get_ipython = lambda: ip
    c = TestClient(app)
    j = c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=' + quote('mine = base + 5')).json()
    assert j['ok'], j
    j = c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=mine').json()
    assert j['ok'] and j['result']['value'] == '15'
    rows = c.get('/agent/api/rows').json()
    names = [n['name'] for g in rows['groups'] for n in g['nodes']]
    assert 'mine' in names and 'base' in names and rows['agent_names'] == ['mine']
    assert 'mine' not in ip.user_ns                     # agent names never touch the owner namespace

def test_agent_exec_policy_blocks():
    ip = _IP({'base': 10, 'items': [1, 2]})
    B.get_ipython = lambda: ip
    c = TestClient(app)
    for bad in ('del items', 'items.append(3)', 'items[0] = 9', 'import shutil; shutil.rmtree("/nope")'):
        j = c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=' + quote(bad)).json()
        assert not j['ok'] and j['error'].startswith('blocked:'), (bad, j)
    assert ip.user_ns['items'] == [1, 2]

def test_agent_shadow_leaves_owner_intact():
    ip = _IP({'base': 10}); B.get_ipython = lambda: ip
    c = TestClient(app)
    assert c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=' + quote('base = 99')).json()['ok']
    assert ip.user_ns['base'] == 10
    assert F.agent_layer()['base'] == 99                # and the owner can see what the agent did
    j = c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=base').json()
    assert j['result']['value'] == '99'

def test_agent_readonly_forces_isolated():
    F._agent_mode = 'readonly'
    j = TestClient(app).get('/agent/api/exec?code=' + quote('ro = 1')).json()
    assert j['ok'] and j['scope'] == 'isolated' and 'ro' not in F._agent_session().layer
    F._agent_mode = 'restricted'

def test_agent_mode_off_403():
    F._agent_mode = 'off'
    for path in ('/agent/api/rows', '/agent/api/exec?code=1', '/agent/api/info'):
        assert TestClient(app).get(path).status_code == 403
    F._agent_mode = 'restricted'

def test_agent_info_reports_contract():
    j = TestClient(app).get('/agent/api/info').json()
    assert j['mode'] == 'restricted' and 'blocked' in j['policy'] and isinstance(j['agent_names'], list)

def test_owner_token_gates_owner_endpoints():
    import paar.runtime as RT, types
    class _Sh:
        def __init__(s): s.user_ns = {}
        def run_cell(s, code, store_history=True):
            exec(code, s.user_ns)
            return types.SimpleNamespace(result=None, error_in_exec=None, error_before_exec=None, success=True)
    sh = _Sh(); RT.get_ipython = lambda: sh
    F._token = 'sekret'
    try:
        c = TestClient(app)
        assert 'owner token required' in c.post('/exec', data={'code': 'w = 1'}).text and 'w' not in sh.user_ns
        assert c.get('/api/exec?code=w%3D2').status_code == 403
        r = c.post('/exec', data={'code': 'w = 1'}, headers={'X-Paar-Token': 'sekret'})
        assert r.status_code == 200 and sh.user_ns['w'] == 1
        assert c.get('/api/exec?code=1%2B1', headers={'X-Paar-Token': 'sekret'}).status_code == 200
        assert 'X-Paar-Token' in c.get('/').text        # owner UI carries the token for its own htmx calls
        j = c.get('/agent/api/exec?code=' + quote('t = 1')).json()
        assert j['ok']                                   # the agent surface never needs the owner token
    finally:
        F._token = None

def test_write_token_roundtrip():
    p = F._write_token(9321)
    try:
        assert p.read_text() == F._token and (p.stat().st_mode & 0o777) == 0o600
    finally:
        p.unlink(); F._token = None

for t in [test_agent_exec_creates_and_reads, test_agent_exec_policy_blocks,
          test_agent_shadow_leaves_owner_intact, test_agent_readonly_forces_isolated,
          test_agent_mode_off_403, test_agent_info_reports_contract,
          test_owner_token_gates_owner_endpoints, test_write_token_roundtrip]: t()
```

## The app

Inline CSS + hljs/CodeJar editor scripts, and the module state every
route shares. The same `app` serves four frontends: the inline Jupyter
iframe, the browser, `paar-tui` (JSON API), and agents (`/agent/api`).

### Owner token & agent session

Two privilege tiers, enforced server-side. **Owner** endpoints that
mutate the real namespace (`/exec`, `/set`, `/api/exec`) are gated by a
random per-server token, written to `<reg_dir>/token-<port>` (0600): the
server-rendered UI embeds it in its htmx headers and `paar-tui` reads
the file, while agents are never handed it. **Agents** get a
lazily-created
[`AgentSession`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/agent.html#agentsession)
(see `agent`) — reads fall through to the owner namespace, writes stay
in the agent’s layer, every cell passes the restriction list.
[`agent_layer()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/fasthtml.html#agent_layer)
is the owner’s window into what the agent has built; token gating is a
courtesy lock against well-behaved local tools, not a cryptographic
boundary (see the security notes in the index).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L228"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### agent_layer

``` python
def agent_layer():
```

*Names the connected agent has created/shadowed -\> their values;
inspect it live from your own kernel.*

## Rendering

[`VarInfo`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/core.html#varinfo) rows
become htmx fragments: lazy `<details>` toggles for containers, paged
grid tables for arrays/frames, click-to-edit values, and the CodeJar
exec bar with completion.

## Owner routes (HTML)

The htmx-facing routes behind the web panel. `/exec` and `/set` check
the owner token when one is armed; read-only routes stay open (they
serve the same data the JSON API exposes).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L464"

## JSON API

The frontend-agnostic feed `paar-tui` consumes: same bridge, no HTML.
`/api/exec` is owner-gated like its htmx twin; `/api/envs` is the
discovery feed for env switchers.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L512"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

``` python
def api_session(
    name:str=''
):
```

*Code-cell sources for a saved session as JSON.*

``` python
def api_rows(
    profile:str=None
):
```

*Grouped node tree for a profile as JSON — the frontend-agnostic feed
the terminal UI (paar.tui) consumes.*

## Agent surface: `/agent/api`

What `paar-mcp` (and any agent-side client) talks to. Same row/grid
shapes as `/api/*`, but everything flows through the
[`AgentSession`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/agent.html#agentsession)
overlay + policy, and exec scope obeys the server’s `agent=` mode:
`restricted` allows the persistent overlay, `readonly` forces isolated,
`off` 403s the whole surface. No owner token is ever required here —
restriction, not authentication, is the control.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L554"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### agent_exec

``` python
def agent_exec(
    code:str, scope:str='overlay'
):
```

*Run agent code under the restriction list; scope=‘overlay’ persists the
agent’s names, ‘isolated’ discards writes (‘readonly’ servers force
it).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L549"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### agent_grid

``` python
def agent_grid(
    accessor:str, roff:int=0, coff:int=0
):
```

*A grid page at accessor in the merged view, or null if not gridable.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L544"

### push

``` python
def push(
    name
):
```

*Push a COPY of owner var `name` into the isolated worker (a pickle copy
– your object is never shared). None on success, else an error string.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L635"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### kernel_rows

``` python
def kernel_rows(
    profile:str=None
):
```

the isolated agent – rendered copies; nothing here can edit owner
state.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L614"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### kernel_exec

``` python
def kernel_exec(
    code:str
):
```

*Run agent code in the process-isolated kernel under paar’s restriction
policy; returns {ok, stdout, error}. Separate from /agent/api (the
in-process overlay) – this never touches the owner’s namespace.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L606"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### kernel_info

``` python
def kernel_info():
```

*Isolated-kernel contract: liveness, env label, ear-marked names, and
the policy (separate process; escapes/exec blocked; owner objects
unreachable; read-only view of shared owner vars).*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L588"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### share

``` python
def share(
    *names, add:bool=False
):
```

*Ear-mark which owner variables the isolated agent may see (read-only
snapshots). No args returns the current allowlist; names set it
(add=True extends); share(None) exposes all.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L569"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### agent_kernel

``` python
def agent_kernel():
```

*The process-isolated agent kernel (a supervised clikernel worker), or
None if not started.*

## Live refresh & server lifecycle

WS broadcast fans a re-fetch nudge to every connected frontend —
`post_run_cell` drives it in-kernel, a poll+diff thread drives it
headless.
[`inspector()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/fasthtml.html#inspector)/[`serve()`](https://vedicreader.github.io/paar/fasthtml.html#serve)/`paar-serve`
are the three entry points; both accept `agent=` and `token=` (see
above) and register in the local registry for discovery.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L766"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### serve_main

``` python
def serve_main(
    argv:NoneType=None
):
```

*Console entry point: `paar-serve script.py [args...]` — run a script
under a live paar inspector, then keep the server up.*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L750"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### serve

``` python
def serve(
    port:int=8000, ns:NoneType=None, name:NoneType=None, poll:float=0.5, profile:str='standard',
    agent:str='restricted', token:bool=True, agent_kernel:bool=True
):
```

*Start the paar inspector in a background thread for a plain
(non-Jupyter) process; returns its base URL.*

ns defaults to the caller’s module globals; name defaults to the
repo/package/cwd label. Refresh is driven by a poll+diff thread, so any
other environment can point paar-tui (or a browser) at the returned URL.
agent: ‘restricted’ (default) serves the policy-guarded /agent/api
overlay, ‘readonly’ forces isolated agent exec, ‘off’ disables the agent
surface. token=True mints an owner token (written to
<reg_dir>/token-<port>) gating /exec, /set and /api/exec.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/vedicreader/paar/blob/main/paar/fasthtml.py#L736"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### inspector

``` python
def inspector(
    port:int=8000, height:int=520, profile:str='standard', name:NoneType=None, agent:str='restricted',
    token:bool=True, agent_kernel:bool=True
):
```

*Start the in-kernel live inspector panel (Jupyter/IPython) and return
the inline iframe.*

agent: ‘restricted’ (default) serves the policy-guarded /agent/api
overlay, ‘readonly’ forces isolated agent exec, ‘off’ disables the agent
surface. token=True mints an owner token (written to
<reg_dir>/token-<port>): /exec, /set and /api/exec then require it – the
owner UI and paar-tui pick it up automatically, while agents stay on
/agent/api.
